runs.summary: 1 new idea, 2 new connections logged, 1 resolved
runs.content:
**Investigation idea:** Micron has no Finance idea page despite being flagged as structurally advantaged in AI & Tech's own supply-chain research. data-hbm-supply-chain identifies Micron as the fastest HBM share gainer (4%→21%) with HBM sold out through 2026–2027, and the AI ecosystem constraint-edge model ranks Micron ahead of CoreWeave. Yet Micron only exists in the wiki as a volatile paper-trading position (MU, repeatedly flagged 'critical watch' with thin stop buffers) — no idea-amd.md-style fundamental page synthesizing the structural bull case. Next step: run /equity-analyst MU.

**Cross-domain connection 1:** AI & Tech's cognitive-decline research and Health's dementia-prevention protocol describe the same neural mechanism from opposite directions, unlinked. Tom Slater's piece (source-slater-ai-mind-2026) cites neuroscience showing the anterior mid-cingulate cortex 'grows with effortful challenges' and that passive AI use removes that friction. Dr. Tommy Wood's brain-health piece (source-wood-brain-sharp-2026) independently lists cognitive stimulation/processing-speed training as a modifiable dementia-risk factor. Neither page cites the other — directly relevant to Fred given he uses AI tools heavily, and health/my-protocol.md is still a stub.

**Cross-domain connection 2:** Slater's 'apprenticeship crisis' (AI absorbing junior roles eliminates on-the-job learning for humans) and Dwarkesh's 'AIs learning on the job' essay (the next AI paradigm is models themselves learning via RLVR generalisation, in-context, the way a new hire ramps up) are mirror-image arguments never cross-linked — if AI's on-the-job learning bet pays off, it undercuts Slater's proposed human escape hatch.

**Resolved:** The 2026-07-06 Meta Compute / idea-crwv item — idea-crwv.md now carries a substantive See Also cross-reference to the Meta Compute data/model pages with a full risk sentence, even though the MONITOR verdict body itself wasn't rewritten.

**Investigation idea:** Meta Compute scenario model (model-meta-compute-release-market-impact-2026-07-05) explicitly flags idea-crwv.md for an update that hasn't happened — CoreWeave's page still shows only the 2026-06-27 MONITOR verdict with no mention of the Jul 1 Meta Compute news or the model's Bear-case neocloud risk (customer concentration, contract non-renewal). Next step: fold in the model's investment implications (avoid/underweight near-term; watch MSFT/GOOGL/AMZN 2027 capex guidance language as the Neutral-to-Bear trigger).

**Cross-domain connection:** Finance's own fraud-detection framework (concept-short-selling.md's Hindenburg red-flags checklist) has never been turned on Fred's own Crypto holdings. Two of his six Crypto portfolio positions, $LUCKY and $TEST, show exactly the pattern the checklist is built to catch — CertiK security ratings of 3.5/10 (both) and −89%/−98% drawdowns from ATH — with no dedicated company page explaining thesis or continued-hold rationale, unlike $EV and $SDEX which do have company pages. The rigor Fred applies to shorting other people's frauds has never been pointed at his own bag.
